summary: The Docker-based reproduction clones `n8n@1.123.21`, verifies the mounted checkout, starts a vulnerable n8n instance plus a loopback-only HTTP target, creates a low-privilege member account, and confirms that the member can read the loopback target through `/rest/dynamic-node-parameters/options`.
expected_security_impact: A `global:member` user should be able to force the server to request `http://127.0.0.1:9000/secret` from inside the container and receive the loopback-only JSON response `[ {"name":"ssrf-ok","value":"secret-from-loopback"} ]` through the API response.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned `n8n@1.123.21` at commit `7762bef712ee7da86b5d84f23212463e7a745064`, built the reproduction image, started n8n, created an owner, invited a `global:member`, accepted the invite, and the member's `POST /rest/dynamic-node-parameters/options` request returned `[ {"name":"ssrf-ok","value":"secret-from-loopback"} ]`. The run emitted the success marker `EVOHUNT_SSRF_OK` and wrote the same output to `reproduction/last-run.log`.
verification_method: The reproduction container verifies that `/target-repo` is the pinned vulnerable commit, confirms that the exploit runs as a `global:member` account, issues the SSRF trigger, and checks that the exact returned option value is `secret-from-loopback`. The run only succeeds if that exact value is observed and the success marker is printed.
evidence_produced_by_run: The returned API body in the run log includes `[ {"name":"ssrf-ok","value":"secret-from-loopback"} ]`.
The run log records that the exploit was executed from a `global:member` account and that the mounted checkout matched commit `7762bef712ee7da86b5d84f23212463e7a745064`.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned `n8n@1.123.21` tag, verifies the resulting commit, and mounts that checkout read-only into the container at `/target-repo`.
